docs.text: Dialogic Resonator Documentation Dialogic Resonator (DR) is a project funded by the UCL Data Empowered Society Grand Challenge. DR uses artificial intelligence to facilitate decision-making by people on complex societal issues. The project aims to make the use of AI more transparent, accountable, and equitable in participatory decision-making processes. This section provides a high-level overview of DR and its current use cases, without implementation details. Read first Project Overview Current use cases Use case 1: Lendlease Use case 2: IGP (UCL Institute for Global Prosperity) Use case 3: Sidara
docs.title: Home

docs.title: Current use cases

docs.text: Use case 2: Post-conflict rebuilding This use case considers how DR can support decision-making in post-conflict rebuilding contexts, with current attention on Ukraine in collaboration with the UCL Institute for Global Prosperity (IGP). See also the Project Overview and Use case 1: Lendlease . Reconstruction requires balancing urgent delivery with legitimacy, local participation, and long-term resilience. The aim here is to support clearer, more accountable multi-stakeholder dialogue across government, institutions, and communities. Key perspective Make difficult priorities explicit so trade-offs can be discussed openly. Support coordination across actors with different mandates and constraints. Help improve confidence in shared decisions by clarifying how conclusions are reached. Why this matters In recovery settings, trust and social cohesion are as important as technical delivery. A transparent process for discussing priorities can help reduce friction, support collaboration, and contribute to more durable outcomes. Current scope This page presents a general high-level framing. Operational methods and implementation details are intentionally out of scope for this overview.

docs.text: Use case 1: Lendlease The Lendlease use case focuses on the pre-planning-application step in urban redevelopment, where choices are still being shaped and different stakeholders may see priorities differently. See also the Project Overview and Use case 2: IGP . The goal is to support better conversations before formal planning submissions. This includes helping participants surface trade-offs clearly, discuss competing concerns openly, and identify where alignment is possible. Key perspective Encourage more transparent discussion of options and their implications. Improve shared understanding between institutions, professionals, and affected communities. Strengthen trust by making reasoning clearer, not just outcomes. Why this matters Urban redevelopment decisions often carry long-term consequences for neighborhoods, public services, and local opportunity. A more structured and inclusive dialogue process can help reduce misunderstanding and improve confidence in the direction taken. Current scope This summary covers the pre-planning-application phase of decision-making at a general level. Detailed governance, data controls, and technical procedures are maintained in internal and project-specific documentation.

docs.title: Status

docs.text: Project Overview Dialogic Resonator (DR) is a UCL Grand Challenge research project that uses artificial intelligence to facilitate decision-making by people on complex societal issues. It aims to make the use of AI more transparent, accountable, and equitable in participatory decision-making processes. At its core, DR is about improving the quality of dialogue around difficult choices. It aims to help institutions and communities make trade-offs more visible, discuss them more constructively, and build greater confidence in how decisions are reached. DR is being developed through real-world collaborations where multiple stakeholders need to work across different interests, values, and forms of evidence. For a quick entry point to this section, see the documentation home . What DR is trying to change Decision processes that are hard for non-specialists to follow. Consultation settings where some voices dominate and others are missed. Conversations that focus on outcomes without clearly showing the reasoning behind them. Current focus areas DR is currently framed through two active use cases: Urban redevelopment decision support focused on the pre-planning-application step through the Lendlease use case . Governance support for post-conflict rebuilding through the Ukraine use case , in collaboration with the UCL Institute for Global Prosperity. A third use case with Sidara is in development and will be expanded as the scope is defined.
